summary: postinstall hook exfiltrates host identity to webhook.site collector
details: On `npm install`, the package's `postinstall` script (postinstall.js) executes `whoami` via `child_process.exec` and POSTs the output, stderr, error, and timestamp via `https.request` to the hardcoded URL `https://webhook.site/0ea9eb45-3ede-4cf0-9ea9-2b8d700272e7`. This fires automatically as part of the npm lifecycle, leaking the installer's OS username to a third-party collector endpoint. The package advertises itself as a 'date formatting utility' but ships no library code (declared `main: index.js` is absent from the tarball); the only effect of installing the package is the recon beacon. The deceptive description, empty author, and unrelated exfiltration payload are consistent with a malicious recon/PoC package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
